rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Allied Heating And Air Conditioning Co., Inc. Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 27, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Allied Heating & Air Conditioning Co., Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) For instance, Defendant asked no questions regarding Plaintiff's alleged exposure at the Metropolitan Life Building in San Francisco from 1972 to 1973. (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Constr. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 81 [courts "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) Plaintiff's objection to Defendant's Exhibit L (printout from the California Secretary of State's website) is sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Nelson V. Goodrich, Et Al - Defendant Allianz Notice Of Motion And Msj
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 27, 2019 in Department 503, Line 1. Intervenor Allianz Insurance Company, on Behalf of Its' Suspended, Insured, Defendant Sudgen Engineering Co.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826.) For instance, Defendant asked no follow up questions regarding Plaintiff's testimony that the name Sudgen rings a bell. (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Constr. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 81 [courts "can infer nothing at all with respect to questions which were neither asked nor answered"].) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: DALAL METWALLY VS. AVON PRODUCTS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction; Declaration Of Elizabeth N. Branham
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 27, 2019 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Avon Products, Inc.'s Motion to Quash Service of Summons for Lack of Personal Jurisdiction is continued to August 27, 2019, pursuant to Plaintiff's request to conduct jurisdictional discovery, which is granted. The discovery is limited to personal jurisdiction in California as indicated in items 14-26 in Plaintiff's opposition (Opp. at 14). Supplemental opposition, if any, due by August 12, 2019. Supplemental reply, if any, due by August 19, 2019. If a hearing is requested to contest this continuance, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
